# Poststratification by high-dimensional features


::: {style="font-size:1rem;"}

::: {.fragment}
- High-dim feature set

| Symbol | Meaning |
|---|---|
| $f_1$ | Marriage status (unmarried, married, after 1st marriage) |
| $f_2$ | Environment (agrarian, trading, fishing) |
| $f_3$ | Sexual risk behaviour (age-adjusted lifetime partners) |
| $f_4$ | Socio-economic status of household |
| ... |
| ... |
:::

<div style="margin-top:3em;"></div>

::: {.fragment}
- Poststratification:

<div style="font-size:1.5rem;">
$$
\mathbb{E}(z_{fg}) = \sum_{i,j} p( c_{ij} = 1 | \text{data} ) \: \mathbb{1}\{i \in f\} \: \mathbb{1}\{j \in g\} \: \zeta^{-1}_f \: \zeta^{-1}_g
$$
</div>
:::

::: {.fragment}
- Iteratively include features with conditional mutual information
:::

:::
